channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Naliendele Stars crowned Nanauka CRDB Cup champions after penalty win over Sokoni FC
item.description: Naliendele Football Club were crowned champions of the Nanauka CRDB Cup after a thrilling 4–2 penalty shootout victory against Sokoni FC at Nangwanda Sijaona Stadium in Mtwara. The intense final ended...

item.title: Norbert Ndjeka's Role in Transforming South Africa’s Fight Against Drug-Resistant TB
item.description: Norbert Ndjeka, born in 1965 in what is now Sankuru Province, DRC, has played a pivotal role in transforming the management of drug-resistant tuberculosis (TB) in South Africa. Initially trained in Ki...

item.title: Marcos declares year-long national energy emergency amid Middle East conflict
item.description: President Ferdinand Marcos Jr. on March 24, 2026, declared a state of national energy emergency due to the sharp rise in global oil prices caused by the ongoing war between the United States, Israel, ...

item.title: Maharashtra relocates 25 leopards to Gujarat's Vantara rescue centre amid rising human-animal conflict
item.description: Maharashtra has relocated 25 leopards to Vantara, a wildlife rescue and rehabilitation centre managed by the Reliance Foundation in Jamnagar, Gujarat. State Forest Minister Ganesh Naik informed the le...

item.title: Russia's Recruitment of African Migrants into Its Military During the Ukraine War
item.description: The article highlights how Russia has been exploiting African migrants, especially from countries like Zambia, to fight in the ongoing Ukraine conflict. The first documented African death in the war w...

item.title: Kenya Pushes to Settle Afcon 2027 Hosting Fees Ahead of Deadline
item.description: The Kenyan government is working to resolve concerns about the country's readiness to host the Africa Cup of Nations (Afcon) in 2027, especially regarding the Ksh 3.9 billion hosting fee. Sports Cabin...

item.title: Kilimanjaro Ice Cover Grows to 5.92 Square Kilometres Thanks to Conservation
item.description: The ice cover on Mount Kilimanjaro has shown a remarkable increase, growing from 2.24 square kilometres in 2010 to 5.92 square kilometres in 2025, as highlighted by recent studies. This increase is at...

item.title: Judge Absent As Sowore’s Fundamental Rights Case Against DSS, Meta, and X Postponed
item.description: For Abuja, Nigeria: The Federal High Court wey suppose deliver judgment for the fundamental rights case wey Omoyele Sowore file against the DSS, Meta, X Corp, and the Attorney General, no happen yeste...

item.title: Ontario students plan major protest over changes to OSAP funding
item.description: Queen’s Park in Ontario is preparing for a large-scale student protest against recent changes to the Ontario Student Assistance Program (OSAP). The Ford government announced in February that OSAP fund...

item.title: Researchers Develop Peptide-Based Oral Insulin Delivery System
item.description: For over a century, scientists have attempted to develop insulin pills to replace daily injections for diabetes management, but the digestive system has historically destroyed the insulin before it co...

item.title: PSX gains 1,200 points after Eid holidays, but faces a cautious market
item.description: The Pakistan Stock Exchange (PSX) experienced a significant boost on Tuesday, with the benchmark KSE-100 index rising by 1,225.99 points or 0.80%, closing at 153,966.36. This rally followed the Eid br...

item.title: Ontario privacy commissioner calls on Ford government to reconsider freedom of information changes
item.description: Ontario’s Information and Privacy Commissioner, Patricia Kosseim, has urged Premier Doug Ford’s government to reconsider proposed changes to the province’s Freedom of Information (FOI) laws. She said ...

item.title: ICPC to Face Nasir El-Rufa’i with Multiple Charges in Court
item.description: The Independent Corrupt Practices and Other Related Offences Commission (ICPC) has announced it will arraign former Governor of Kaduna State, Nasir El-Rufa’i, in both the federal and state high courts...

item.title: Delhi Budget 2026: Key Announcements on Education, Skill Development, and Youth Empowerment
item.description: The Delhi Budget 2026 has placed significant emphasis on youth empowerment, education, and skill development, with several key announcements designed to support these sectors. Among the major highligh...

item.title: Ex-cyclone Narelle expected to redevelop near WA coast
item.description: Ex-cyclone Narelle, which previously swept through parts of Australia, is expected to reform off the coast of Western Australia in the early hours of tomorrow. The weather system, which has already ca...

item.title: Sinner Surpasses Djokovic’s Record at Miami Open
item.description: Italian tennis sensation Jannik Sinner has broken a 10-year record held by Novak Djokovic at the Miami Open. Sinner’s straight-sets victory over Corentin Moutet saw him win his 26th consecutive set at...

item.title: JI Chief Calls on Govt to Reduce Petroleum Levies Amid Rising Prices
item.description: On Tuesday, Jamaat-i-Islami Emir Hafiz Naeemur Rehman strongly criticised the federal government over the steep rise in petroleum prices, which he said is putting a heavy burden on ordinary citizens. ...

item.title: Rahul Gandhi Criticizes PM Modi's Foreign Policy, Accuses Him of Being 'Compromised'
item.description: In a recent statement, Rahul Gandhi, the leader of opposition in the Lok Sabha, called Prime Minister Narendra Modi's approach to India's foreign policy a 'personal' policy and dubbed it a 'universal ...

item.title: Dobson urges Stormers to improve decision-making ahead of Edinburgh clash
item.description: Stormers coach John Dobson has called for a reduction in errors and better decision-making as his team prepares to face an improved Edinburgh squad in Cape Town. Despite a hard-fought 29-21 victory ov...

item.title: Niger Tornadoes boost confidence ahead of Abia Warriors game after win over Rivers United
item.description: Niger Tornadoes dey enter their next Nigeria Premier Football League game against Abia Warriors with serious confidence after dem beat Rivers United 1–0 for Minna. The Ikon Allah Boys manage collect t...
